title: Best VPN for Android 2026: Top Picks Tested on Mobile
description: The best VPNs for Android in 2026 offer fast connections, no-log policies, and easy-to-use apps. We tested the top options on speed, privacy, and streaming.
content:
ANDROID USERS FACE A SPECIFIC SET of privacy risks that desktop users often overlook. Mobile apps request permissions aggressively, public Wi-Fi connections are constant, and carrier-level tracking is harder to avoid. A good VPN on Android addresses all three, but not all VPN apps are built equally for mobile.

Best VPN for Android 2026: Quick Summary

NordVPN is the best overall VPN for Android in 2026, combining fast WireGuard speeds, a verified no-logs policy, and a polished Android app. ExpressVPN is the top pick for streaming. Mullvad is best for privacy-first users. Surfshark offers the best value for multi-device households. ProtonVPN is the strongest free option with no data cap.

1. NordVPN (Best Overall)

NordVPN runs WireGuard by default, which delivers the fastest speeds with the lowest battery impact. The app includes a kill switch, split tunneling, and Threat Protection Lite. The no-logs policy has been independently audited multiple times. 6,300 servers across 111 countries. From around .09 per month on a two-year plan.

2. ExpressVPN (Best for Streaming)

ExpressVPN consistently unblocks Netflix, Disney+, BBC iPlayer, and Hulu from more locations than most competitors. The Lightway protocol is fast and stable on mobile networks. From around .67 per month on a one-year plan.

3. Mullvad (Best for Privacy)

Mullvad does not require an email address to sign up. The Android app supports WireGuard, has a built-in kill switch, and blocks ads and trackers through DNS. A flat 5 euros per month with no multi-year discount.

4. Surfshark (Best Value)

Surfshark allows unlimited simultaneous connections on one subscription. The Android app includes split tunneling and NoBorders mode for restrictive networks. From around .19 per month on a two-year plan.

5. ProtonVPN (Best Free Option)

ProtonVPN free tier has no data cap, no speed throttling, and a verified no-logs policy. Limited to three server locations on the free tier. Paid plans from .59 per month.

What to Look for in an Android VPN

Use WireGuard or IKEv2 rather than OpenVPN for lower battery overhead. Check for an automatic kill switch. Split tunneling lets you exclude apps like banking apps from the tunnel. Verify the provider has undergone an independent audit.

FAQs

What is the best VPN for Android in 2026?

NordVPN is the best overall based on speed, security audits, and app quality. For streaming, ExpressVPN is the strongest pick.

Is there a good free VPN for Android?

ProtonVPN free tier is the best free VPN for Android. It has no data cap and a verified no-logs policy.

Does a VPN drain Android battery?

VPNs using WireGuard have minimal battery impact, typically under 3-5% additional drain. OpenVPN-based VPNs drain more.

Can a VPN slow down my Android phone?

Any VPN adds some latency. With WireGuard the difference is usually under 10ms on fast connections.

Does Android have a built-in VPN?

Android has a built-in VPN client but it lacks a kill switch and privacy features. A dedicated VPN app is far more reliable.

What VPN works best on Android for Netflix?

ExpressVPN and NordVPN both unblock Netflix reliably. ExpressVPN has a slight edge for international Netflix catalogs.

Is it safe to use a free VPN on Android?

Most free VPNs monetize through data collection. ProtonVPN and Windscribe are the only free options with credible privacy practices.
